posts.caption:
The European Grand Tour (traditionally taken by young, wealthy British aristocrats between the 17th and 19th centuries) is having something of a revival. For a certain American set, it’s become de rigeur to decamp to the Continent for a new take on extended holidays.
Kris Jenner is a study in how to do it right. While Kim watched boyfriend Lewis Hamilton take second place at Monaco, her momager hotfooted it to Mallorca to spend time on the yacht of billionaire entertainment mogul David Geffen – alongside Ellen deGeneres and Portia Rossi.
The boat is moored on the west of the island, home to the celebrity hotspots of Deia and Sóller, as well as some of its best hotels. Among them, rose-tinted Hotel Corazon is owned by British fashion photographer Kate Bellm and her husband, Mexican artist and horticulturist Edgar Lopez, and sits in the mountains mid-way between the two towns.
It’s a place where you’ll wake to palm-fringed views over the countryside and end the day with a long dinner on the restaurant terrace with orange-hued skies as the backdrop.
From there, guests can navigate squiggling roads towards the pine-scented coves of the coast or meander Deia’s tightly-woven lanes of restaurants and boutiques. Make time for the vintage railway of Ferrocarrer de Sóller, a wooden train built in 1912 that trundles through the countryside towards Palma.
How to do it: Doubles at Hotel Corazon cost from £390 per night, room only
Photo credit: Kate Bellm @katebellm

On paper, @loropiana wool and @gramicci climbing pants have no business sharing the same sentence. Yet thanks to Japanese menswear label @nonnative, the unlikely pairing is back for another round, with Gramicci's rugged climbing pants and shorts cut from Loro Piana's luxurious Super 130's wool.⁠
⁠
This season's fabric is slightly heavier at 260 GSM, creating a sharper silhouette and richer appearance while retaining the easygoing functionality that defines Gramicci. The contrast between refined Italian suiting cloth and practical climbing details remains the collection's biggest draw, proving once again that some of fashion's strangest combinations make the most sense. The collection releases June 20.⁠

Nearly 40 years ago, two Swedish skiers sat on a chairlift in Åre, Sweden, and came up with an idea for a different kind of performance brand. Frustrated by the loud colors and maximalist designs dominating skiwear in the '80s, they envisioned something cleaner, more functional, and rooted in Scandinavian design. That idea became @peakperformance.⁠
⁠
Ahead of the brand's 40th anniversary, we traveled to its birthplace to spend 48 hours hiking, exploring, and testing its latest collection in Åre's famously unpredictable weather. Hit the link in bio to read about the experience.

Designed with @umbro, the collection centers on a new turf boot silhouette manufactured in Sheffield, reconnecting the brand with the city and craftsmanship that helped define its legacy.
Alongside it sits a bespoke textured knitted football kit that channels classic pitch aesthetics with a refined twist.⁠
⁠
Available now on our app.

There’s nothing @levis doesn’t already know about denim jackets, but two recent collaborations with Japanese tastemakers EDIFICE and BIOTOP prove the iconic trucker still has room to evolve. EDIFICE reworks Levi’s 1953 Type II with a lambskin leather collar and subtle leather-lined cuffs, creating what feels like a "leather jacket lite" version of the classic denim staple.⁠
⁠
Meanwhile, BIOTOP takes the Type I in a different direction, coating the jacket in glossy black polyurethane that mimics leather while concealing its denim roots. Together, the projects make a compelling case that the secret ingredient to a great denim jacket might just be leather, or at least something that pushes it beyond the classic denim formula.
Ellos juegan por la copa, nosotras por el handbag ranking ⚽✨ Y chicas... The bags did not disappoint! Cuál es tu favorito? #WorldCup 2026

posts.caption:
The question of whether we can, or even should, separate the art from the artist is one of culture’s most enduring ethical dilemmas. It resurfaces every time an adored musician is exposed for harmful behavior, or a fashion designer’s legacy is re-examined through a more contemporary lens. In today’s hyper-visible, instantly shareable media landscape, the debate has become even more polarized. There’s rarely a neutral position: either you continue to celebrate the work, or you reject it entirely, zero nuance.
Here we unpacked the pattern, or lack of pattern. It turns out we’re far quicker to condemn those whose art we weren’t that invested in anyway. The art and the artist are bound together, whether we like it or not. The least we can do is be honest about when we choose to look away, and ask ourselves who that is really serving.
Head to the link in bio to read the full piece.
Credits: Courtesy of brands and Getty

posts.caption:
For her debut menswear show at @pittiuomo_official 110, @simonerocha_ brought a gentler side to menswear.
Staged inside Florence’s historic Teatro della Pergola, the collection explored Rocha’s vision of the “gentle man,” challenging traditional notions of masculinity through romantic tailoring, exposed underpinnings, delicate fabrics and hand-embroidered details.
In a city synonymous with classic menswear, Rocha offered an alternative perspective: one where softness, vulnerability and strength coexist.
posts.alt_text:
At just 22 years old, @mingsuyi has been carrying the heavyweight title of “Olympic Champion” for four full years. Following his recent victory in Milan this February, where he once again captured the global spotlight, tracing the well-trodden path of mainstream sports media to recount his snowfield legends would be nothing short of a low-efficiency repetition.

This time, we shift the story towards a milestone Su Yiming is revealing for the very first time: his identity as a streetwear brand founder.

Taking center stage of Hypebeast’s latest digital cover story, Su Yiming sits down with us for an in-depth, exclusive conversation. Under the curation of the theme “Made by Ming,” this feature decodes his artistic trajectory by tracing his evolution from “The Builder” to a conscious creator (The Creator), then into a cultural symbol (The Icon). Most crucially, for the first time, this interview reveals his highly anticipated personal brand, Süugar. 
Head over to the link in bio to read the full cover story.   22 歲，蘇翊鳴（@mingsuyi）已經被外界叫了整整四年的「奧運冠軍」。自他於 2 月在米蘭再次拿下冠軍、再度成為全球矚目的焦點後，若是再去重複其他媒體的既定路徑，一遍又一遍地講述他的雪場傳奇，無異於一場低效的重複勞動。 
此次我們將目光轉向蘇翊鳴首次揭露的全新身份 —— 潮流品牌創始人，從 Hypebeast 最熟悉以及最專業的時尚與街頭領域，與讀者們一起重新認識「蘇翊鳴」。
擔綱 Hypebeast 最新一期數位封面人物的蘇翊鳴，今次將與我們展開一場深度對談。整個企劃圍繞著「Made by Ming」這一主題，重新了解他如何從一名建設者（The Builder）發展至創作者（The Creator），再到進化成為文化符號（The Icon）的路徑，並在訪問中首次揭開其個人品牌「Süugar」的神秘面紗。  點擊 Link in Bio  閱讀完整封面故事。  Photographer: Zhongjia Sun @zhongjia.sun⁠
Director: Daniel Teo @_danielteo_⁠

2026 父親節精選禮物指南

今天就是父親節！還沒準備好禮物？別擔心，Hypebeast 編輯部特別為你準備了這份「壓線送禮清單」，最適合今天直接帶父親出門逛街時按圖索驥！

從雨季通勤必備的 On Cloud 6 Waterproof 全黑防水鞋、呼應足球熱潮的國家隊球衣，到以當代視角重塑的 Bottega Veneta 全新大號 Veneto 郵差包，無論他是機能風擁躉、球鞋愛好者，還是追求精緻生活的品味男士，這份清單都能讓他的日常造型與生活風格全面升級。趁著今天帶他去門市試穿、挑選，直接把心意送到位！

Hypebeast 編輯部祝天下父親，父親節快樂！

Photo: Courtesy of Brands

擔綱 Hypebeast 最新一期數位封面人物的蘇翊鳴，今次將與我們展開一場深度對談。整個企劃圍繞著「Made by Ming」這一主題，重新了解他如何從一名建設者（The Builder）發展至創作者（The Creator），再到進化成為文化符號（The Icon）的路徑，並在訪問中首次揭開其個人品牌「Süugar」的神秘面紗。  點擊 Link in Bio  閱讀完整封面故事。  Photographer: Zhongjia Sun @zhongjia.sun⁠

posts.caption:
The U.S. Open is often described as golf's most democratic championship. Thousands of players attempt to qualify every year. In theory, anyone can earn their place. This week, it's been played at Shinnecock Hills.⁠
⁠
That's part of what makes the Hamptons such an interesting backdrop for the championship. From the outside, it's easy to reduce the area to a story about exclusivity: old money, private clubs and sprawling estates hidden behind hedges. And while there's some truth to that image, it obscures everything else happening around it.⁠
⁠
Within a relatively small stretch of Long Island, you'll find century-old institutions like Shinnecock, National and Maidstone, newer clubs like The Bridge and Sebonack, public courses like Montauk Downs and local gathering places like Southampton and Sag Harbor. Different clubs with different histories and different ideas about what golf should be.⁠
⁠
The U.S. Open may be the reason people arrive, but the more interesting story is everything they find once they're there. Head to the link in bio for the full story.⁠
⁠
Photo: @rj_gilbert
posts.alt_text:
@nike arrived in the Hamptons this week with a mobile activation designed around the U.S. Open at Shinnecock Hills.⁠
⁠
The traveling trailer featured displays of the brand's championship-week footwear and apparel, alongside a custom putting experience that incorporated artificial turf greens surrounded by real rough and fescue inspired by the host venue.⁠
⁠
The installation appeared throughout the week at locations including a public driving range near Shinnecock, giving fans a chance to get a closer look at Nike Golf's latest releases while experiencing a small slice of U.S. Open week away from the tournament grounds.⁠
⁠
Michelle Wie West also stopped by the activation, where Nike displayed the special footwear she wore during her final U.S. Women's Open appearance at Riviera earlier this month.⁠
⁠
Photo: @newyorksunshine

posts.caption:
Pac-Man soundtrack intro, Kill Bill outro? What’s clear is that the waist — for more reasons than just clipping a mini bag to it — was the star of @prada’s Spring/Summer 2027 runway show.
While prints clashed across slim-cut pants, vests and turtlenecks, it was the mini bags dangling from belt loops that kept pulling our attention. But there was a lot of attention to the waist throughout, jackets were jackets and tops were cropped, but even trousers sat higher on the body. Many looks featured doubled-up waistbands that revealed sewn-in boxer briefs beneath.
Among the standouts were a sheer trucker jacket and matching pants, along with the show’s closing trifecta of tonal Canadian tuxedos in brown, yellow and maroon.
posts.alt_text:
At just 22 years old, @mingsuyi has been carrying the heavyweight title of “Olympic Champion” for four full years. Following his recent victory in Milan this February, where he once again captured the global spotlight, tracing the well-trodden path of mainstream sports media to recount his snowfield legends would be nothing short of a low-efficiency repetition.

This time, we shift the story towards a milestone Su Yiming is revealing for the very first time: his identity as a streetwear brand founder.

Taking center stage of Hypebeast’s latest digital cover story, Su Yiming sits down with us for an in-depth, exclusive conversation. Under the curation of the theme “Made by Ming,” this feature decodes his artistic trajectory by tracing his evolution from “The Builder” to a conscious creator (The Creator), then into a cultural symbol (The Icon). Most crucially, for the first time, this interview reveals his highly anticipated personal brand, Süugar. 
Head over to the link in bio to read the full cover story.   22 歲，蘇翊鳴（@mingsuyi）已經被外界叫了整整四年的「奧運冠軍」。自他於 2 月在米蘭再次拿下冠軍、再度成為全球矚目的焦點後，若是再去重複其他媒體的既定路徑，一遍又一遍地講述他的雪場傳奇，無異於一場低效的重複勞動。

posts.caption: For Spring/Summer 2027, @paulsmithdesign gave his archives a second life. Revisiting tailoring from decades past, Sir Paul reworked familiar suiting with a looser, more relaxed attitude, revived vintage tie prints across shirting and shorts, and delivered the sense of play that has long defined his work.

posts.caption:
@jennifer.garner *always* knows what to do (and it usually involves sorghum).
Between starring in summer’s buzziest new series, taking her near-billion dollar company public, and turning ‘Pretend Cooking Show’ into a reality, she still manages to be the nicest woman in Hollywood.
Playing a widowed food blogger in @Peacock’s adaptation of @elinhilderbrand’s bestseller ‘The Five-Star Weekend,’ the beloved actress and entrepreneur channels her inner Ina Garten for a not-so-Instagrammable girls’ weekend on Nantucket with co-stars-turned-IRL besties, @chloesevigny, @morereginahall, @gemmachan and @darcycarden, exploring what it means to grow and develop in middle age.
At 54, the Marvel Hero and supermom reflects on her genre-spanning career, being “total besties” with John Cena, her surprisingly vast knowledge of weaponry, plus the responsibility of celebrities to stand up for working locally for InStyle’s Summer Imagemakers Issue at the link in @instylemagazine’s bio now.

posts.caption:
20 years after the release of #DevilWearsPrada, the franchise’s screenwriter-director-producer @abmck’s impact extends far beyond your favorite quotes (yes, even “The Cerulean Monologue”). Her real legacy is writing female ambition in a way that still feels...groundbreaking.
Learn how, over the course of her career, InStyle’s #WomanOfImpact invented legendary characters and conceptualized films and shows that became critically acclaimed and culturally beloved—not just as rom-coms, but as “competence porn”: a celebration of working hard, caring deeply about your work, and being really, really ridiculously good at it—at the link in @InStyleMagazine’s bio now.

posts.caption:
Yes, it’s L’OFFICIEL! Cosa hanno in comune “Dune: Part Two”, “Furiosa: A Mad Max Saga” e la nuova serie Apple TV “Lucky”? Sono tutti ambientati nel deserto—e tutti vedono protagonista @AnyaTaylorJoy. «Mi diverte sempre, perché non capisco cosa ci sia nei miei capelli, nel mio incarnato o nella larghezza dei miei occhi che mi renda adatta a interpretare una creatura del deserto. Eppure la gente adora mettermi lì», confessa Taylor-Joy a L’OFFICIEL. «Avverto una vera affinità con questi mondi».
Dal pianeta brullo di Arrakis in “Dune” al Wasteland che domina “Furiosa: A Mad Max Saga”, la nostra protagonista di giugno continua a ritrovarsi negli scenari più ostili del cinema contemporaneo. «Il deserto possiede una sacralità propria», osserva. Alla domanda su cosa ami di questi spazi desolati, la risposta è immediata: «L’alba e il tramonto, soprattutto. Perché vedi i colori più straordinari e senti di essere l’unica persona sulla terra».
Global Chief Content Officier @giampietrobaudo

posts.caption:
Introducing our global cover star, @anyataylorjoy.
“I’ve always been nomadic by nature,” the British-Argentine actor tells L’OFFICIEL. “Now I’ve just fully embraced it as part of who I am. If I have a couple of days off, I’m always like, ‘Ooh, what’s within driving distance?’ If it’s six hours or less, I’m like, ‘Let’s go. Why not?’”
Fresh off of a recent desert road trip, Taylor-Joy—who has cemented her status as a fashion darling through ambassadorships with @Dior and @TiffanyandCo—is preparing for the release of Apple TV’s ‘Lucky,’ a thriller about a con artist caught in the fallout of a multimillion-dollar heist gone wrong. The role marks another chapter in a career spanning ‘The Witch,’ ‘The Queen’s Gambit,’ ‘Furiosa,’ and ‘Dune.’
In ‘Lucky,’ her character is pursued by the FBI and a ruthless mob boss while searching for something more elusive than freedom: stability. “I was so attracted to playing somebody who was just so desperate to stay still and put down roots,” says Taylor-Joy.
Filmed across the desert and inside Las Vegas’s Caesars Palace, the production left its mark. “I have a real soft spot for Caesars Palace now—we’ve been through a lot together,” she laughs.
At the link in bio, read our full conversation with Taylor-Joy on inhabiting new characters, life on the road, and ‘Lucky.’

posts.caption:
Nato il 21 giugno 1982 — il giorno più lungo dell’anno. Il Principe William compie oggi 44 anni, e quest’anno il suo compleanno cade nel Father’s Day: due ruoli che si sovrappongono, due identità che lui porta con la stessa misura silenziosa. Figlio di Diana, che gli ha insegnato che la corona può avere un cuore. Marito di Catherine, accanto a cui ha attraversato momenti che nessun protocollo reale aveva previsto. Padre di George, Charlotte e Louis, per i quali sta costruendo, giorno dopo giorno, un’idea diversa di cosa significhi essere re.
Photo: Getty Images
#PrinceWilliam #RoyalFamily #LOfficiel #LOfficielItalia

posts.caption:
Oggi celebriamo i 41 anni di @honeymoon con una gemma dal nostro archivio: la cover de L’OFFICIEL (aprile 2013) e l’editoriale firmato da Nicole Nodland che ha contribuito a definire l’immaginario di un’artista capace di trasformare nostalgia, romanticismo e malinconia in un linguaggio visivo immediatamente riconoscibile.
Più di una cantante, Lana Del Rey è diventata un riferimento culturale che continua a influenzare moda, cinema e fotografia, costruendo un’estetica senza tempo sospesa tra glamour hollywoodiano e poesia contemporanea.
Photo: L’OFFICIEL ARCHIVE
#LOfficiel #LanaDelRey #WECELEBRATE #LOfficielArchive

posts.caption:
È ufficiale. Loris Messina (@messinaloris) e Simone Rizzo (@simoneri) sono i nuovi Direttori Creativi di @Moschino, con effetto immediato. Fondatori di @Sunnei — il brand milanese che hanno lasciato a settembre 2025 dopo dieci anni e un addio messo letteralmente all’asta da Christie’s — raccolgono oggi l’eredità della maison che Franco Moschino ha costruito sull’irriverenza, sul pop come strumento critico e sul rifiuto di prendere la moda sul serio. Il loro debutto alla guida creativa di Moschino sarà a settembre 2026, durante la Milano Fashion Week. Due designer che si sono venduti all’asta. Ora dirigono la maison.
Photo: Moschino
#Moschino #Sunneij #LOfficiel #LOfficielItalia

posts.caption:
Welcome to the June issue, where L’OFFICIEL explores travel not as movement, but as a state of mind. Our “nomadic by nature” cover star @AnyaTaylorJoy discusses her love of road trips, filming the new Apple TV series ‘Lucky’ in the Las Vegas desert, and her unique approach to learning her characters. The enigmatic photographer @SzilveszterMako, renowned for his surreal images, cinematic backdrops, and dreamlike scenes, turned his lens on the actress, capturing her shifting world for our shoot.
Elsewhere, the issue focuses on slow travel and the art of pausing. We take you to Grand Cayman, Ischia, and Costa Rica, where hydrotherapy and relaxation define the experience, and across the Canadian Rockies, Arabian Desert, and Australian Outback by rail—the defining way to travel in 2026. Elsewhere, we follow one writer into the next frontier of luxury wellness: sleep optimization.
In Paris, we sit down with Hong Kong–born couturier @RobertWun, who has made a name for himself with dramatic, over-the-top silhouettes; he reminds us that slowing down is sometimes the most radical imaginative act. And in Tbilisi, it’s the creative class who are putting the Georgian capital on the map—and we have your guide to everything that’s happening now.
On the fashion front, Spring/Summer 2026’s finest looks head to Cape Town for a sun-drenched escape, while long-heralded summer heroes—unexpected denim and easy dresses—capture the spirit of going nowhere in good company, this time in Los Angeles’s canyons. Stay tuned for our issue to hit newsstands this month.

posts.caption:
@Moschino has announced the departure of creative director @adrianappiolaza after two and a half years at the helm of the Italian fashion house. The Argentinian designer joined the brand in 2024 from Loewe, where he spent a decade as ready-to-wear design director, and previously held roles at Chloé under Clare Waight Keller, Louis Vuitton under Marc Jacobs and Miu Miu under Miuccia Prada.⁠
⁠
At Moschino, Appiolaza brought a fresh perspective to the house's signature humor and irreverence, blending its maximalist codes with sharper tailoring and a more wearable approach. ⁠
⁠
Photography by Stefani Galuzzi and Getty Images⁠
⁠
#Moschino #Adrianappiolaza

posts.caption:
@Balenciaga dropped its Spring Summer 2026 preview collection in Mumbai last night, and the energy in the room felt different.
With Fatima Sana Sheikh, Bobby Deol, Mouni Roy, Sanya Malhotra, Malavika Mohanan, and Krithi Shetty in attendance, it felt less like a formal showcase and more like a real moment around fashion. The room was sharp, observant, and engaged.
Fatima Sana Shaikh said it best: “Balenciaga always has a chic approach and the brand is now heading towards it even more aggressively.” And that tone carried through the evening. Nothing felt loud for attention, yet everything commanded it.
What stood out was how people responded. There was curiosity, closer inspection, and genuine interest in the details. Some silhouettes felt familiar but more refined, while others brought a quiet sense of drama without trying too hard.
That is when a collection works.
Not when it demands attention, but when it earns it.
@balenciaga #Balenciaga

posts.caption:
Jacob & Co. continues to blur the line between watchmaking, sculpture, and fantasy with the Astronomia “Gates of Olympus.”
Priced at $1.4 million, the one-of-one creation draws inspiration from Greek mythology, featuring Zeus, Olympus, celestial movement, and a fully mechanical miniature world built directly into the timepiece itself.
Inside the 18K rose gold case sits a triple-axis tourbillon, over 9 carats of baguette diamonds, and an intricate Olympus scene designed as part of the movement.
More than a watch, the piece reflects how modern haute horology is increasingly moving toward collectible art and immersive storytelling.
@JacobAndCo #JacobAndCo #Luxurious #Collab

posts.caption:
“Dubai it” has become more than a phrase. It’s a mindset.
The city’s latest philosophy encourages people to think bigger, move faster, and approach challenges with the ambition that transformed Dubai from a desert trading post into one of the world’s most influential luxury, business, and tourism destinations.
Whether it’s architecture, technology, hospitality, or lifestyle, the idea is simple: if something can be imagined, Dubai believes it can be built. And often, built bigger than anywhere else in the world.
#DubaiIt #Luxurious

posts.caption:
From Bugatti and Rolls-Royce to Pagani and Ferrari, the world’s most expensive cars are far more than transportation. They are rolling works of art, engineering masterpieces, and symbols of extreme exclusivity.
Built in tiny numbers and often tailored to their owners’ exact specifications, these hypercars command prices that can exceed the cost of private jets, superyachts, and luxury estates. For collectors, rarity is just as valuable as performance, turning the world’s most coveted automobiles into some of the most sought-after assets in luxury.
In a world where exclusivity defines value, these cars represent the absolute pinnacle of automotive craftsmanship.
#Luxurious

posts.caption:
#ManifestCover
There is a quiet power to Rukmini Vasanth (@rukmini_vasanth). One that doesn’t seek attention, yet holds it effortlessly.
For Manifest’s May–June cover, she steps into an imaginative world where temple jewellery meets contemporary silhouettes, blurring the lines between memory and modernity. She embodies a woman in transition, grounded, luminous, and yet constantly becoming.
“I’m not very good at describing myself,” she says, almost in passing.
Yet, in the same breath, her choices reveal a quiet clarity of intent. “I don’t want to isolate my character. I need to understand the whole world it exists in.”
Between restraint and reinvention, stillness and movement, she exists in that rare in-between. Not fully defined, not confined. Just on the cusp of a wider sky.
Our latest issue is now live on Magzter. Read the full cover story from the link in bio.
Chairperson: Avarna Jain (@avarna_jain)
CEO: Debashish Ghosh
Editor: Aindrila Mitra (@aindrilamitra)
Interview: Akshay Kaushal (@akshay_kaushal)
Photography: Vaishnav Praveen (@vaishnavpraveen)
Styling: Mohit Rai (@mohitrai)
Fashion assistant: Shubhi Kumar (@shubhi.kumar)
Hair: Rohit Bhatkar (@rohit_bhatkar)
Makeup: Riviera Lynn (@rivieralynn)
Video: Kris Arshad (@itskrisarshad)
PR Agency: Tree-Shul Media (@treeshulmediasolutions)
Artist management: Benchmark Talents (@benchmarktalents)
Production: Studio Little Dumpling (@studiolittledumpling)
On Rukmini: pantsuit, Kanika Goyal Label (@kanikagoyallabel); earrings, bangles, and rings, Gehna Jewellers (@gehnajewellers1)
An @rpsg.lifestylemedia publication by @rpsggroup
#Manifest #manifestcover #rukminivasanth
posts.alt_text:
There’s nothing a gang catch-up can’t cure; from big work-life rants and wedding planning stress to last-minute shopping runs and endless to-do lists. But between all the chaos, making time for your favourite people isn’t always easy.

Luckily, if you’re in Chennai, here are the newest restaurants, bars, and rooftop hotspots that are perfect for those much needed catch-ups. Whether you’re in the mood for craft cocktails, live jazz, comforting food, or stunning city views, we’ve got every vibe covered.

Read more via the link in bio 🔗
By Ashwin Rajagopalan (@curatedbyashwin)

#Manifest #Food
[Chennai Nightlife, Pre-Wedding Catch-Ups, Wedding Planning, Restaurants, Bride Squad, Groom Squad, Food, Restaurants and Bars, Squad]

posts.caption:
For Milind Soman (@milindrunning) and Ankita Konwar (@ankita_earthy), yoga and fitness aren’t activities you make time for—they’re a way of life.
Ahead of International Yoga Day, Ankita Konwar opens up about the role yoga has played in her life and why it continues to be one of her most important wellness practices. She believes it’s one of the best ways to reconnect with your body, especially in today’s fast-paced world where physical and mental wellbeing often take a backseat.
The couple’s shared love for movement extends beyond their daily routines and into their travels too. Whether it’s running, yoga, hiking, or exploring nature, Ankita says active holidays help them unwind, recharge, and strengthen their bond.
Read the full interview via the link in bio 🔗
Words: Ismat Tahseen (@ismat.tahseen)
#Manifest
[International Yoga Day 2026, Mental and Physical Health, Couple Goals, Relationship]

posts.caption:
Wedding morning starts with a hundred emotions running through the mind, it’s easy to feel overwhelmed before one of the biggest moments of your life.
This International Yoga Day, celebrity yoga trainer Rupal Sidhpura Faria (rupal_sidh) shares simple rituals that can help couples feel calmer, more present and grounded before they start their life together. From pranayama and Surya Namaskars to mindful eating, self-massage and candle gazing, these practices aren’t just about looking radiant—they’re about stepping into your next chapter with intention.
Because before the makeup, the photographs and the celebrations, the most important thing is how you feel within.
Link in bio🔗
#Manifest #InternationalYogaDay
[Bridal Glow, Mindful Bride, Wedding Planing, Stressed, Feel Calm, Good Health]

posts.caption:
Weddings come with their fair share of last-minute chaos. But if we had to turn to a fictional father for guidance, there are a few TV and movie dads we’d trust without hesitation. Be it navigating pre-wedding nerves, family dynamics or simply needing someone to remind us what the day is really about.
These iconic fathers have spent years showing us what it means to love your partner and your family. They’ve taught us that love isn’t just found in grand gestures—it’s in showing up every day and choosing the people you love, over and over again.
#Manifest #FathersDay
[Fathers, Father’s Day, Wedding Planning, Relationship Goals, Relationship Advice, Love Stories, Pop Culture]

posts.caption:
As a designer of women’s clothing for 15 years, Simone Rocha has been committed to presenting a version of womanhood that subverts traditional feminine tropes, a practice she calls “pretty and perverse.” But as the culture reaches its peak “masculinity crisis” moment, @simonerocha_ is now turning more of her attention to men’s wear — focusing more on men’s softer side.
“When I started thinking about the men’s very seriously, I wanted it to feel grounded but also quite tender,” she told the New York Times reporter @yolamzizi.
Rocha has been designing men’s clothing since 2022, though until now, they have mostly served as counterpart to the women’s collections. Now she's giving men's wear its own space. This week, Rocha presented her first solo men’s show at Pitti Immagine Uomo, the biannual Italian trade show for men’s clothing. Will men bite?
Tap the link in our bio to read our full profile of Rocha and learn more about the collection came together. Photo by @jeremiesouteyrat
posts.alt_text:
Knicks fans showed up in all sorts of creative combinations of orange and blue during the ticker-tape parade on Thursday, but perhaps no one turned the merch into quite as much of a fashion statement as Rama Duwaji, the first lady of the city.

Making a rare public appearance with Mayor Zohran Mamdani — himself in a Josh Hart jersey under his suit jacket, with a polka-dot orange tie — Duwaji chose a one-shouldered peplum top by Claire Sullivan, an emerging New York designer. It was made from upcycled Knicks T-shirts in white, orange and blue, and she wore it with a full black skirt, orange pompom earrings and black lace-ups with ankle socks. But the top, which was a sort of downtown Les Miz-meets-DIY-meets-team spirit confection, was the point.

Tap the link in our bio to read more about how @ramaduwaji's parade look came together. Photo by Yuki Iwamura/@apnews

Rocha has been designing men’s clothing since 2022, though until now, they have mostly served as counterpart to the women’s collections. Now she's giving men's wear its own space. This week, Rocha presented her first solo men’s show at Pitti Immagine Uomo, the biannual Italian trade show for men’s clothing. Will men bite?

Tap the link in our bio to read our full profile of Rocha and learn more about the collection came together. Photo by @jeremiesouteyrat

posts.caption:
After tabloid reports claimed Taylor Swift would wed Travis Kelce on June 13 in Watch Hill, Rhode Island, fans descended upon the town hoping for a glimpse of the singer.
The reports, though unconfirmed, seemed plausible, some fans said. The singer, a numerology fanatic, has a longstanding fondness for the number 13; a June wedding would work with Kelce’s offseason; and, perhaps most convincingly, Swift has owned a home, known as Holiday House, in Watch Hill since 2013.
While no Swifts were to be found last weekend, there were, however, plenty of Swifties. Fans of all ages, including sisters, mother-daughter duos and one group on a trip with “three generations of Swifties,” embraced the charms of the town. Read more about how Swift fans spent their weekend in Watch Hill at the link in our bio. Photos by @sarahmeftah

posts.caption:
The fifth annual Las Culturistas Culture Awards, created and hosted by the comedians Bowen Yang and Matt Rogers, were held at the United Theater on Broadway in Los Angeles on May 30. The show aired last night on Bravo and is now streaming on Peacock.
An offshoot of the hosts’ popular “Las Culturistas” podcast, the celebrity-packed comedy special is both a parody of the self-seriousness of awards shows and a loving tribute to the nooks and crannies of pop culture that traditional ceremonies ignore. Spanning more than 100 categories, the awards recognized humans, foods, cartoons and abstract ideas.
Stacey Rusch of “The Real Housewives of Potomac” beat Mayor Zohran Mamdani for best new artist. Ciara Miller, a fan favorite from “Summer House,” won this year’s “Allison Williams cool girl award.” Eric Nam, a singer and recent contestant on “The Traitors,” won most surprising snack, toppling competitors that included “Big thing of cottage cheese.”
“Here at the Culture Awards,” Yang stressed onstage, “there is no such thing as high or low culture. Everything is just culture.” At the link in our bio, read more about the Culture Awards winners and see more photos from inside the show (and the after-party). Photos by Bravo, Rodin Eckenroth/Getty Images, @alexwelshphoto

posts.caption:
Last week, Ellie the Elephant, beloved mascot of the New York Liberty, celebrated her sixth birthday party. It was a bash involving dance routines and costume changes, held in front of 17,000 people at Barclays Center. Many elements of Ellie’s birthday outfit and party were provided by sponsors. But to her fans, Ellie is far more than a billboard.
Over the past six years, Ellie’s distinctive aesthetic and courtside panache have allowed many Black women, Liberty fans or not, to see themselves in her. In performance, she has been known to channel divas like Mary J. Blige, Lil’ Kim and Whitney Houston. “Ellie represents culture,” Criscia Long, senior director of entertainment for the Liberty, told the New York Times reporter @s_evangelina.
At the link in our bio, see more scenes from @bigellieliberty’s birthday bash and read more about what the mascot means to fans. Visuals by @djdumpling

posts.caption:
A reader asked our fashion critic: "I’m 52 years old. During my life, jeans fashion has gone from bell bottoms to acid-wash to ripped to boot-cut to low-rise to skinny to high-rise to mom jeans to ripped again to wide-leg. … Please make it stop. I don’t have another jeans transition left in me. Is there a timeless jeans style for those of us who simply cannot keep up?"
The options for denim can be overwhelming. At the link in our bio, @vvfriedman offers advice on how to choose the style that works best for you. Photo by Getty Images

posts.caption:
For the grand finale of her wedding to #CallumTurner, #DuaLipa looked to none other than #Chanel for her wedding dress. The custom #HauteCouture creation marked a historic moment for the house as the first-ever bridal look by #MatthieuBlazy.
The dress which was made in Paris was entirely hand-embroidered with 480,000 beads by @ateliermontex. It featured a two-meter train which was underlined with 25,000 feathers by @maison_lemarie
Images courtesy of @davidsimsofficial
posts.alt_text:
I fear #ChristianLouboutin has taken the whimsy trend to a whole new level, transforming one of the most iconic visual gags (the banana peel slip) into a shoe.

Made of yellow satin, the $1,995 heels feature hand-sculpted leather panels which curl around the foot to resemble the folds of a peeled banana. The design is a homage to the footwear of #IsabelCanovas who became renowned for transforming fruit, vegetables, flowers, shells, and other everyday objects into wearable sculptures. Much like her creations, the #Louboutin heel elevates something ordinary and  slightly absurd into luxury. #Shoes #FootwearDesign

Images courtesy of @styledbyjovon_ @christianlouboutin & The Metropolitan Museum of Art

posts.caption:
#Balenciaga Spring 2027 may be #PierpaoloPiccioli’s clearest vision for the brand yet. Less about direct references, it focuses on #CristóbalBalenciaga’s philosophy of construction and the relationship between fabric and the body.
References to the house’s historic balloon, drape, and cocoon shapes suggest a welcome return to its heritage, but reengineered for modern life. Through new textile innovations, the collection emphasises lightness, with entire looks weighing less than a kilogram. @pppiccioli wants to demonstrate how #HauteCouture principles can be relevant to everyday life. Ordinary wardrobe staples are elevated through couture-level construction.
What do you think of the new Balenciaga so far?

posts.caption:
This has to be one of my favourite editorial shoots I've seen this year, and that's probably because it was shot by #SzilveszterMako, whose images feel closer to theatre and painting than traditional fashion photography.
His editorials often have a dreamlike quality because of their painterly approach. Through dramatic lighting, strong contrasts between light and shadow he gives his subjects an almost sculptural presence. The clothing blends seamlessly into the composition, contributing to the overall mood and becoming an extension of the subject rather than something just being worn.
#Rihanna shot by @szilvesztermako for @ee72, styled by @edward_enninful wearing #Schiaparelli #Chanel #Dior Alexander McQueen & Bottega Veneta

posts.caption:
One of my favourite, and most hauntingly beautiful details from #DanielRoseberry’s #Schiaparelli couture collections was the sheer crinoline veils/face masks, from Fall 2024. Wrapped around the face and head they functioned as sculptural forms. The material appeared almost weightless, creating distortions that blurred the contours of the face and transformed the models into embryonic works of art. Makeup by @patstingz, hair by @guidopalau
Also reminded me of #GarethPugh’s Spring 2013 collection, which featured sheer mesh and stretched fabrics wrapped around the head and face, or #MarcJacobs Spring 2019. Major inspo for brides looking for an unconventional wedding veil!

posts.caption:
If you have any of these bags, please step forward… #KarlLagerfeld was known for his larger-than-life shows, and the spectacle didn’t just stop at the sets, but translated into his absurd yet highly collectible bags.
Think of the iconic hula hoop bag from Spring 2013, which Karl joked was designed for the beach so you could stick it into the sand and hang your towel from it (which fyi someone is currently selling on eBay for £145K.) Then there was the supermarket show, complete with milk carton bags and the #Chanel chain basket, or the Spring 2019 flap bag filled with sand.
The bags became souvenirs from those fantasy worlds and now #MatthieuBlazy is continuing that tradition, turning handbags into collectible objects that blur the line between fashion and art.
Which one is your favourite?

This is a real piece of history for us all. The past 10 years of lessons and learning have led up to this moment and I couldn’t be happier to share it with you all.

PRINT ISN’T DEAD.

posts.caption:
Travis Scott for Issue 01: “The First Ever” Outlander Magazine In-Print!
GANG, TRAVIS SCOTT IN HERMÈS FOR OUR FIRST EVER MAGAZINE COVER. THIS IS UNHEARD OF, AND BETTER YET? IT WAS FOR OUTLANDER MAGAZINE. I AM HUMBLED.
WOW, what a moment. My only wish in this life is that what I’ve spent the last 10 years building acts as a strong form of inspiration for your own creative careers - I came from no fashion background, I had no connections & I had no idea what I was doing, but, I did it anyway.
If you didn’t know by now, my name is @callum.mccaff , I founded Outlander - and I’m here to remind everyone that PRINT ISN’T DEAD.

posts.caption:
India's Renee Noronha has won first place in the 18-24 category at the Ironman Philippines. Completing the gruelling challenge at 15:55:37, the 20-year-old IIT Madras student upheld held her record of being the youngest Indian female Ironman finisher.
Renee battled torrential rains and unforgiving temperatures to complete the race. In conversation with SheThePeople, the triathlete also revealed that she participated on day 3 of her period, making the victory a profound reminder of her strength and resilience as a woman.
With this win, Renee has now qualified for the Ironman World Championship Hawaii, scheduled for October 2026. She is also on a mission to set a new Guinness world record to become the youngest person in the world to do a full Ironman on 6 continents. She is on track to achieve this by April 2027, at the age of 21.
Read full interview at www.shethepeople.tv. | @renee.noronha
#Ironman #Philippines #sports full Ironman challenge, women in sports, women in endurance sports, mumbai sports, Chennai sports, IIT Madras student, women in STEM, ironman Kona, triathlon, marathon, menstrual awareness
posts.alt_text:
FIFA has officially selected and accredited Assamese photojournalist Gitika Talukdar to cover the upcoming 2026 FIFA World Cup. This will be her third time covering the Men's FIFA World Cup.

Prior to this, Gitika has covered the 2018 Russia World Cup, the 2022 Qatar FIFA World Cup, the 2019 France Women's FIFA World Cup, the 2023 Women's FIFA World Cup in Australia and New Zealand, the 2020 Tokyo Olympic Games, the Paris Olympic Games, and others.

Visit www.shethepeople.tv for more

#FIFA #worldcup #photography sports photography sports photojournalist sports journalist indian media india at FIFA World Cup 2026 north america football news soccer news

posts.caption:
Yoga knocked on Nikita’s door before acting. When preparing for Miss India, Nikita started to do yoga as a mere means to get fit, but ended up getting incredible results.
Nikita, an accomplished actor, swears by yoga, as it’s the only thing that keeps her going. Her daily routine consists of powerful yoga practices, including cardio exercises and meditation. Nikita's dedication to yoga is evident, looking at her public appearances and social media posts.
In a conversation with SheThePeople, Nikita Dutta shares about her yoga journey, self-rituals that help her keep going, and defines the role of yoga in her life.
Visit www.shethepeople.tv to read the full interview by @_shivanilamba
#yogaday #yogalife #nikitadutta International Yoga Day, Nikita Dutta, actress, bollywood, yoga se hoga, ek dooje ke vaaste, exercise, yoga practices, spirituality, mental health, yoga poses, daily rituals, trending, viral, explore, dedication, social media, Instagram

posts.alt_text:
India's Renee Noronha has won first place in the 18-24 category at the Ironman Philippines. Completing the gruelling challenge at 15:55:37, the 20-year-old IIT Madras student upheld held her record of being the youngest Indian female Ironman finisher.

Renee battled torrential rains and unforgiving temperatures to complete the race. In conversation with SheThePeople, the triathlete also revealed that she participated on day 3 of her period, making the victory a profound reminder of her strength and resilience as a woman.

With this win, Renee has now qualified for the Ironman World Championship Hawaii, scheduled for October 2026. She is also on a mission to set a new Guinness world record to become the youngest person in the world to do a full Ironman on 6 continents. She is on track to achieve this by April 2027, at the age of 21.

Read full interview at www.shethepeople.tv. | @renee.noronha

#Ironman #Philippines #sports full Ironman challenge, women in sports, women in endurance sports, mumbai sports, Chennai sports, IIT Madras student, women in STEM, ironman Kona, triathlon, marathon, menstrual awareness

The bride’s dress was entirely hand-embroidered and required around 1,155 hours of needlework, while the groom wore a custom classic black suit by Louis Vuitton.

posts.caption:
Some love stories begin with vows. This one began with a touch to the forehead, rose petals still falling around them.** ✨
This wedding by the lake felt like something out of a dream a domed mandap dressed entirely in white blooms, water stretching out behind it, and two people sitting close enough that the world outside the petals simply disappeared.
There was a softness to it all. The kind you can’t stage: in the bride’s hand reaching up to touch his face, in the groom’s fingers gently brushing her hairline, in a sea of ivory and blush garlands swaying between them as confetti drifted down like it had nowhere else to be.
This wasn’t just an outdoor ceremony. It was two people choosing each other in the open air, with the lake as their only witness.
Captured exactly as it unfolded the rose petal shower, the locked eyes, the laughter caught mid-turn, and the magic of forever beginning right there on the water’s edge.
Bride & Groom: @tejasdhoke11 @ishpreet_dang
Venue: @savanalakeresort
Outfit (Bride): @rimpleandharpreet
Outfit (Groom): @muktiandkavithcasa

posts.caption:
Fashion styling is a job. Not a hobby. Not a passion project. Not something people do on the side because they love clothes.
It is a profession that takes years to build. The eye. The contacts. The knowledge of how the industry actually works across every sector, every brief, every type of client. That does not come from loving fashion. It comes from years of showing up, doing the work and figuring it out mostly alone.
And yet it is still one of the most undervalued roles in the creative industry.
Stylists are asked to work for free. For credit. For exposure. For a rate that does not come close to reflecting the skill, the hours or the experience involved. And somewhere along the way the industry decided that was normal. That stylists should be grateful for the opportunity. That loving what you do means you should not expect to be paid properly for it.
We are past that.
Behind every iconic image, every red carpet moment, every campaign that stopped you mid-scroll, there was a stylist. Building the looks, interpreting the brief, managing the pressure, absorbing the chaos so nobody else had to. Often without a credit. Sometimes without a proper rate.
That changes when stylists know their worth, charge it without apology and stop accepting the jobs that treat their career like a favour.
➡️➡️ If you are a working fashion stylist who is ready to do exactly that, comment STUDIO and we will send you the free trial link to The Studio of Stylist Elixir. Rate guides. Contracts. Portfolio tools. Masterclasses every Monday. Everything you need to build a career that reflects what you are actually worth.
Save this. Send it to a stylist who needs to hear it.
posts.alt_text:
Jonathan Anderson just showed his first couture collection for Dior. If you do not understand why it mattered, this post is for you.

John Galliano was front row. The artisans were the opening act. The silhouettes came from ceramicist Magdalene Odundo.

If you are in Paris: the show space at Musée Rodin opens to the public from 28 January for one week. The new couture collection, archival Dior pieces, and the Odundo ceramics that inspired it. You can see it yourself.

This is not about liking or not liking a collection. This is about understanding what you are looking at.
The Studio of Stylist Elixir is our online membership. Guides. Templates. Live calls. A community of stylists who are learning and building together. Comment ‘studio’ for a free trial.

Save this. Share it with someone who needs it.

#dior #jonathananderson #stylistelixir

posts.caption:
Hey everyone.
Ralph Lauren just gave a styling masterclass in Milan, and the lesson is in the detail.
It opened lakeside, in golden-age Italian-racing glamour, silk suits and featherlight ties, then turned to campus prep, scribbled and paint-splattered. A dinner jacket patched in boro and sashiko denim with the Japanese house Kuon. Edwardian neckwear. Madras, a South Indian cloth from Chennai, layered check on check. It closed on a hand-embroidered patchwork varsity jacket, heritage taken apart and rebuilt.
None of that reads unless you know what you are looking at. The study behind the eye is what a client feels when they trust you, even if they could never name it. Talent gets you in the room. The knowledge gets you booked.
What is one show that taught you something? Tell me below.
This is exactly what we go deeper on inside The Studio. Comment STUDIO to come and learn with us.

posts.caption:
If you’ve styled for more than a week, you’ve heard at least three of these. Usually with a smile, usually from someone who means well. And it still chips away at you.
Here’s the thing though. The clients who say these aren’t your people. Your dream clients, the ones worth building your whole business around, would never. So instead of trying to convince the wrong ones, the last few slides are how you talk about your worth and start booking the ones who already get it.
Which one are you most tired of hearing? Drop the slide number below.
When you’re ready to name your dream clients and go after them properly, comment BOOKED and I’ll send you my free Dream Client worksheet.
Day 11/20
#fashionstylist #personalstylist #fashionstyling #stylistlife #stylisttips
posts.alt_text:
Fashion styling is a job. Not a hobby. Not a passion project. Not something people do on the side because they love clothes.

It is a profession that takes years to build. The eye. The contacts. The knowledge of how the industry actually works across every sector, every brief, every type of client. That does not come from loving fashion. It comes from years of showing up, doing the work and figuring it out mostly alone.

And yet it is still one of the most undervalued roles in the creative industry.

Stylists are asked to work for free. For credit. For exposure. For a rate that does not come close to reflecting the skill, the hours or the experience involved. And somewhere along the way the industry decided that was normal. That stylists should be grateful for the opportunity. That loving what you do means you should not expect to be paid properly for it.
We are past that.

Behind every iconic image, every red carpet moment, every campaign that stopped you mid-scroll, there was a stylist. Building the looks, interpreting the brief, managing the pressure, absorbing the chaos so nobody else had to. Often without a credit. Sometimes without a proper rate.

That changes when stylists know their worth, charge it without apology and stop accepting the jobs that treat their career like a favour.

➡️➡️ If you are a working fashion stylist who is ready to do exactly that, comment STUDIO and we will send you the free trial link to The Studio of Stylist Elixir. Rate guides. Contracts. Portfolio tools. Masterclasses every Monday. Everything you need to build a career that reflects what you are actually worth.

Save this. Send it to a stylist who needs to hear it.

posts.caption:
Here’s the part of the job nobody sees.
You just play with pretty clothes all day, right?"
If I had a pound for every time someone said that to me. The bit everyone sees, the nice outfit, the finished shoot, is the tiny part right at the end. The real job is the weeks of sourcing, the relationships, the chaos you quietly absorb so no one else has to, and the eye it took years to build.
And here's the thing I want you to really hear. All of that is skilled work, and it deserves to be paid like it.
Tell me below, what's the part of your job you most wish people understood?
When you're ready to stop waiting to be chosen and actually book the clients you want, comment BOOKED and I'll send you my free Dream Client worksheet to start naming them.

posts.caption:
Your sun, moon and rising are still fighting for the aux.
#WorldMusicDay
posts.alt_text:
Summer has always shaped life in India. What is changing is the extent to which life now has to be organised around heat.

People are leaving work earlier, staying out later, sleeping on floors, changing their diets, adjusting their school attendance, and spending more of their income on cooling. 

These decisions are often framed as adaptation, yet many emerge from necessity rather than choice. For those who work outdoors, miss a day’s wage if they stay home, or cannot afford air conditioning, there is little room to negotiate with rising temperatures.

Heat leaves few dramatic images behind. It does not wash away roads or flatten buildings. Its effects accumulate in the body, in lost income, disrupted sleep, worsening health and shrinking margins for recovery.

As hotter days are followed by hotter nights, how long can individual households keep absorbing a crisis they did not create? 

Read more at the link in bio.
By Arshia Dhar (@urseaa)

#HeatwaveIndia #ClimateCrisis #ExtremeHeat #ClimateChange #SummerInIndia

posts.caption:
Delhi’s microbrewery boom did not arrive overnight. Licensing delays, high production costs and a beer market long shaped by commercial labels meant the city came late to craft brewing.
That delay is now part of the story. Delhi’s newer microbreweries are not just putting wheat beer on tap; they are trying to build a more curious beer culture, one that makes room for seasonal brews, local ingredients, barrel-aged stouts, sours and beers with a point of view.
Craft beer is still far from the norm in India. But in Delhi-NCR, it is beginning to move beyond novelty, becoming part of a more experimental, experience-led way of drinking.
Read more on the link in bio.
By Karishma Kuenzang (@kkuenzang)
#CraftBeerIndia #MicrobreweriesInDelhi #DelhiBreweries #BeerCulture #TheEstablished

posts.caption:
Celebrity make-up artists in India have been shaping faces, trends and bridal moodboards for years. The beauty boom is here. The artist-led empire is still missing.
Globally, names like Charlotte Tilbury, Pat McGrath and Bobbi Brown turned artistry into authority, and authority into billion-dollar beauty businesses. In India, the same conversion has been slower. Local artists have built trust across skin tones, textures, climates and undertones, often doing the kind of real-world R&D global brands ignored for years.
They know what oxidises on brown skin. They know what collapses in Mumbai humidity. They know what survives a wedding, a shoot and a close-up.
Yet many remain tied to the wedding economy, where bridal bookings offer faster, steadier returns than the long, expensive work of building a brand. The result is a strange beauty paradox: India has the talent, the market and the demand. What it still lacks is the machinery.
Read more at the link in bio.
By Ria Bhatia (@riabbhatia)
#makeupbrandsinindia #indianmakeupbrands #beautyindustry #celebritymakeupartists #colourcosmetics

posts.caption:
Celebrating the rise of the Godawan began with a simple question: how does a bird return to public memory?
A bird that builds its nest directly on the ground is especially vulnerable when people keep taking over its habitat. Once considered a candidate for India’s national bird, the Great Indian Bustard, locally known as Godawan, has gradually disappeared from public consciousness over the decades due to habitat loss and human encroachment.
Today, its conservation story is gaining renewed attention. In Rajasthan, breeding and hatchery programmes have brought cautious progress, with new hatchlings emerging in protected facilities. Conservation efforts combine habitat restoration, protected nesting areas, water management, predator control, captive breeding, and community-led monitoring of breeding grounds.
On World Environment Day, one of India’s most iconic monuments became a canvas for this conservation story, as Qutb Minar hosted projection mapping that highlighted the Godawan’s ecological role in India’s grassland ecosystems and the challenges facing its survival, with support from Godawan Estuary Premium Water.

posts.caption:
The Fashion Calendar can look like a mess out of context, but it is effectively the blueprint for the entire industry. And being able to understand and implement the language, systems, and strategy of it all is ultimately what’s going to keep any aspiring fashion professional in the game. If you can’t read “FW25 RTW” and know what you’re looking at (or ideally be able to picture the collection in your mind’s eye if you know the designer and director), it can be easy to feel like you’re lost.
And you can’t afford to get lost in fashion. So without further ado, here’s your cheat sheet to understanding how the Fashion Calendar actually works, all of its essential terminology, and why. And yes, I made a calendar chart you can save and reference.
Read the latest on Substack: The Citizen’s Poste | posteposteposte.com
#FashionCalendar #Substack #Couture #Menswear #FashionCapitals
posts.alt_text:
Chanel has officially revealed the details behind Dua Lipa’s custom Haute Couture wedding gown, handcrafted in the ateliers at 31 Rue Cambon under the direction of Matthieu Blazy. The design took over 1,000 hours and brought together some of Chanel’s most celebrated Métiers d’Art, with Atelier Montex, Lemarié, and Lesage contributing intricate embellishments, while custom white satin pumps were created by Massaro.

And hey… the signs may have been there all along. Dua fronted the Chanel 25 campaign, has maintained a close relationship with the house for years, and famously wore vintage Chanel bridal-inspired couture to the 2021 Met Gala.

But most notably, this marks the first Chanel Haute Couture wedding dress ever designed by Matthieu Blazy, created not for a runway finale, but for a friend of the House.

#DuaLipa #ChanelBride #MatthieuBlazy #CallumTurner #Chanel

posts.caption:
Are we all good right now? Do we all need a drink right now? Love is apparently in the air, it’s finally the weekend, and yet fashion continues to refuse the concept of rest. Between surprise weddings, honeymoon photos, designer (bridal) debuts, and enough celebrity news to power the timeline for days… it’s been a week.
Unfortunately for all of us, the pace isn’t slowing down anytime soon. Men’s Fashion Week is officially here in Milan and Paris, which means more collections, more opinions, and more screenshots filling up my camera roll.
Until then, enjoy the weekend. Touch some grass. Have a drink. Fall in love if you must.
#FashionWeek #DuaLipa #MilanFashionWeek #ParisFashionWeek #MatthieuBlazy

posts.caption:
Babe, wake up! Dua Lipa finally posted the wedding photos—and she’s officially a Chanel bride. For the grand finale of her wedding to Callum Turner, Dua looked to Chanel for a custom haute couture gown, marking a historic moment as the first bridal look designed by Matthieu Blazy for the house.
Handcrafted in Paris, the gown was entirely hand-embroidered with an astonishing 480,000 beads by Atelier Montex and finished with a dramatic two-meter train edged in 25,000 feathers. Equal parts craftsmanship, romance, and fashion history.
The internet is going to be staring at these photos for the foreseeable future.
#DuaLipa #CallumTurner #ChanelBride #MatthieuBlazy #HauteCouture

posts.caption:
I’m an editor first, but I’m also an interiors girl. That combo means there are very few things I enjoy more than a good showroom, and @realisationpar’s flagship at 95 Mount Street might be my favorite place in London…to shop, at least.
The new flagship feels like stepping into someone’s very well-lived-in, very well-dressed apartment. Designed as a collaboration between founders Alexandra Spencer and Teale Talbot and designer Sophie Baker, the space leans into this idea of a “sensory archive” rather than traditional retail.
Upstairs, it’s all soft light, antique furniture, and layered textures while downstairs, a dressing-room-meets-private-salon with mirrored walls, rich fabrics, and just the right amount of drama.
You’ve got to stop by.
#RealisationPar #LondonShopping #RetailDesign #FashionRetail #StoreDesign

posts.caption:
I’m not sure @simon_porte_jacquemus has ever missed when it comes to runway locations, but this might be one of his best yet. For Spring/Summer 2027, @jacquemus will present “Le Bonheur” at Phare de la Pietra, a historic lighthouse perched on a rocky island off the coast of Île-Rousse in Corsica.
It marks a return to those sweeping, site-specific natural landscapes the brand does so well.
The show will take place June 29 at 10 a.m. local time, so be ready to tune in on Instagram if your invitation got lost in the mail.
And it goes deeper than just the visuals: Jacquemus is hosting Corsica-wide castings to feature local models and partnering with the town to help restore the lighthouse.
A runway, but also a gesture.
#Jacquemus #RunwayShow #FashionWeek #SS27 #FashionCulture

posts.caption:
with the 2026 World Cup in full swing, we can’t stop thinking about football’s most famous superfan: Rihanna.
whether she was cheering for Brazil from the stands or celebrating Germany’s victory right on the pitch, Rihanna has a habit of finding herself at the centre of football’s biggest moments.
#TFO #rihanna #football
posts.alt_text:
for spring/summer 2027, Miuccia Prada and Raf Simons stripped menswear down to a series of conscious choices. cropped knits, transparent trousers, exposed waistlines and slimmer proportions transformed familiar wardrobe staples into something more revealing.

at a time when menswear often relies on oversized silhouettes, armour-like layers and displays of power, Prada moved in the opposite direction. clothes became lighter. proportions became smaller. what was usually hidden was left exposed.

in Prada’s world, vulnerability might just be the new luxury.

#TFO #prada #runway #menswear

posts.caption:
there’s something undeniably sexy about eyeliner that’s lived a little.
somewhere along the way, we convinced ourselves that beauty should be neat, precise, and untouched. but the best makeup never looks like it just left the vanity. it looks like it survived a night out, a cigarette break, a good sweat and a dance floor at 2 a.m.
perfect eyeliner says you arrived. smudged eyeliner says you’ve got a story. and honestly, we’re choosing the story every time.
#TFO #makeup #beauty #eyeliner #messymakeup

posts.caption:
a father’s day campaign from Harry’s is asking a surprisingly simple question: are we checking in on the wrong generation?
according to the brand’s commissioned YouGov research, 82% of fathers say hearing from their child would give them a mood boost. to bring the campaign insights to life, Harry’s teamed up with Asim Chaudhry to launch the Feel Good Hotline, a public installation encouraging people to pick up the phone and reconnect.
a timely reminder during Men’s Health Week that sometimes the smallest gesture can have the biggest impact.
#TFO #harrys #fathersday #beauty #menshealth

posts.caption:
Fatherhood is a new role for the actor who first got noticed for his performance in ‘Masaan’ (2015). In fact, it seems this off-camera Vicky Kaushal (@vickykaushal09) is perhaps closer to Deepak Chaudhary, the character he played in the film. A sensitive, ambitious young man coming to terms with the world around him. That character, Kaushal says, changed him. But not for the obvious reasons.
“Until then success, to me, was where you reached [in life]. It was never about where you started from,” he explains. “That character, starting from the crematorium ghat in Benaras and going on to work as an engineer in the Railways in Allahabad [now Prayagraj]... He had a far bigger life arc than I’ve covered in my life. Till today,” he says, tapping the arm rest for emphasis. “After that I started having so much empathy for those around me, especially those from far less privileged backgrounds, because I don’t know where they have started from.”
@travellerprasad in conversation with the Bollywood actor at the link in bio.

posts.caption:
Thirty minutes after this interview was supposed to begin, Vicky Kaushal’s (@vickykaushal09) tall, statuesque frame bursts in through the door at the Matrix office in Khar, Mumbai. “So sorry, bro,” he says, shaking this reporter’s hand with both of his. His day has been busy—his son’s visa was to be sorted, he couldn’t leave his wife alone, and there’s repair work going on in his building, he says, sounding like your average young husband and father with his daily household chores to take care of, not some Bollywood star with a platoon of staff to handle his every whim.
Since becoming a father, Kaushal says he’s come to understand and appreciate “a lot more” of the work his parents did in raising him. “It occurs to me at the most random moments that that [instilling the right values] is now my responsibility towards my son,” he says. “He is going to learn by observing how his parents are thinking, talking, and behaving. So, I’ve realised whatever you want to see reflected in him has to come in your actions rather than words.”
@travellerprasad in conversation with the Bollywood actor at the link in bio.

posts.caption:
Only the best for my baby 😩 🐕🧴
A new generation of pet-care brands with the aesthetics and language of fancy skincare has emerged in the last couple of years, selling pet shampoos with polished, luxurious packaging that might make you reconsider your own shower shelf.
Of course, the boom makes perfect sense when you consider how most people think about their pets. If you have one, it’s likely that you consider them a full-fledged member of your family with their own bed, birthday celebrations, and a better social life than you. Whether you’re looking to upgrade your pooch’s routine or looking for the perfect gift for a dog-obsessed friend, see @chloechou13 and @heerj_’s list of  the most luxurious dog shampoos on the market right now.

posts.caption:
Size does matter when it comes to Delhi’s bar scene, but probably in reverse. The city’s nightlife is getting smaller by design, flipping the old logic that bigger is better.
After XS spaces like the 20-seater The Love Hotel (@thelovehotelbar) in Lodhi Road and the nine-seater Cavity, part of Barbet & Pals (@barbetandpals) in GK-2 (a bar within a bar, because apparently one bar was not enough), we’ve now got—drumroll—a four-seater.
Yes. Four. Some SUVs can seat more people than this bar.
The Hidden Club (THC) by Olive (@olivedelhi), accessed through a backdoor in Olive Qutub in Mehrauli, has just four seats. It is essentially the group’s beverage experimentation space that they’ve now opened to the public.
At the link in bio, @geetikasachdev all about the XS bar.

posts.caption:
What happens when an embroiderer interprets the work of a miniaturist? New Delhi-based Dr Rohini Arora has spent decades exploring the answer to that question, first through academic fieldwork and then a brand she called Rumals of Chamba (@rumalsofchamba), which specialises in their namesake Chamba rumals, a traditional form of textile art that originated in the hill regions around Chamba, Himachal Pradesh.
What distinguished Arora’s research was its focus not just on documenting the Chamba rumal’s history but on understanding its technical evolution. By the time she completed her PhD in 2015, Arora had amassed more than 120 research objects—painstaking recreations that demonstrated the technical and aesthetic nuances she had uncovered.
The turning point arrived during the Covid-19 pandemic, when, upon her husband’s prompting, she decided to transform years of scholarship and experimentation into a formal enterprise. Rumals of Chamba emerged not as a revival project but as a continuation of a body of research decades in the making.
At the link in bio, @tanya.91 chats with Arora on her brand, and the craft that inspired it.

The Atlas Of Affluence, the definitive white paper on the Rise of Indian Luxury and what it means across categories, culture, craft and new models of aspirations. Launching today.

posts.caption:
One morning, I woke up to an email telling me that Kai had been paralysed and skinned alive for a handbag. He had spent his life in a putrid pit, in a cesspool of faeces, fighting for space until the day he was immobilised with blunt force and killed.
Kai is a crocodile. The email came from People For Ethical Treatment of Animals (PETA).
This reminded me of one of fashion’s lingering contradictions. We celebrate craftsmanship and luxury, yet rarely ask what they sometimes cost. Despite avowals by many luxury brands to eliminate fur and exotic skins, the industry continues to depend on animal leather for handbags, shoes and accessories. Fashion journalism has made a promotional religion of sustainability, but animal welfare remains at the margins.
Every year, at events across the world, activists protest against the use of feathers, exotic skins and other animal-derived materials. They make headlines for a day but the questions they raise fade away quickly.
Since I am deeply distressed by animal cruelty, I first argued with myself before discussing this with the TVOF team. Was I biased?
Whether or not one agrees with every PETA campaign is beside the point. The questions surrounding leather deserve scrutiny. One of fashion’s most enduring defences is “ethically sourced leather”. Animal rights organisations argue there is no such thing. Leather is not merely a by-product of the meat industry, but a highly profitable industry in its own right. Reptiles are killed for their skins and bovine leather remains intertwined with cattle slaughter. The global leather goods market was estimated to be roughly $471 billion in 2024, almost matching the global beef market. Buying leather, thus, sustains an economic system that places commercial value on animal skins.
This is not to condemn everyone carrying a leather handbag. It is to invite compassion from an industry that has demonstrated ingenuity through recycled fibres, mushroom and pineapple leather and lab-grown materials.
June marks Save Their Skins Month. Perhaps it is time to ask questions about supply chains that bring animal leather to luxury ateliers.
Image 1 courtesy @peta

posts.caption:
Hi, I’m Veronica 👋🏼 a sewing pattern designer based in Melbourne, Australia.
I create patterns, tools and resources that help you sew with understanding.
My work focuses on how fabrics behave, how garments are constructed, and how sewing connects us to the history of what we wear.
Sewing Patterns
My patterns are designed for versatility, wearability and fun. They include the Hazel, Freya, Odette, Audrey, Hera, Cordelia and the Sleeve Studio — each combining comfort with thoughtful design details. You’ll also find The Noelle, a free beginner-friendly pattern that introduces my approach to fit and construction.
The Paper Doll
A print-at-home paper doll designed to help you visualise and play with my sewing patterns. You can cut it out and colour it yourself, or use the pre-filled versions featuring my fabric prints and colour palettes. The set includes curvier model options and three skin tones to choose from.
The Conscious Consumer eBook
A practical guide to fabrics, sustainability and sewing with intention. It helps you understand fibre origins, weave structures and how to make long-lasting choices when you sew or buy clothes.
Digital Sewing Tools
Two paid calculators designed for precision:
• Shirring Calculator – works out fabric shrinkage and elastic thread length.
• GSM Calculator – estimates fabric weight and drape.
Available individually or as a discounted bundle.
My PDF sewing patterns are stocked digitally through @fabricgodmother , @spotlightstores , @thefoldline , @joans.finefabric and @thedrapersdaughter .
Fabric and Accessories
You can shop my fabric designs on @spoonflower and find my illustrated phone cases at @thedairy .
Everything I create is designed to help you sew with confidence, understanding and creativity.
For all enquires email us at hello@veronicatuckerthelabel.com

posts.caption: About textile crafts - magical power - women: When you have a look at the old fairytales of Germany, France and Northern Europe, these stories are also filled with that pattern. In the "Nibelungenlied" (the story of Sigfried the Dragonslayer, who gets to bath in dragonsblood which makes him uninjurable besides a very small spot on his shoulder and later gets killed through that spot by Hagen von Tronje) Krimhild, Sigfrieds Wife wants to protect him and markes the spot on his tunic with red thread. Also in the beginning of the story she and her women (she is a princess, so this is her household staff) are described to make lots of clothes for the men, also to embroider them. They did it for protection, also for presenting their social status.

posts.caption:
Every culture created a goddess who presided over making, but not all of them did it the same way.
This edition introduced two categories.
The first: goddesses whose mythology is explicitly about weaving (documented, primary sources, this is what the texts actually say).
The second: goddesses that weavers chose: figures of creativity, wisdom and skill that women at the loom reached toward deliberately, even though weaving wasn’t the centre of their story.
Both categories are true, just in different ways.
And here is what I keep coming back to across all three editions of the First Makers series. You never find a GOD of weaving. Not once, across any culture. Every time, the act of making cloth belongs to the feminine divine.
That is the oldest memory we have of what women’s knowledge meant.
Full episode on The Culture of Cloth podcast 🎧 link in bio. Start there if you’re new here.
Save this and share it with someone who makes things.
Cover art: Court Ladies Preparing Newly Woven Silk, a Chinese silk painting by Emperor Huizong of Song, early 12th century.

posts.caption:
Six months of research, four in-depth online sessions and one of the oldest stories ever told 📕
I’m joining @readingsbooks Big Book Club for a deep dive into Homer’s Odyssey. It’s an online session running every Thursday from 18 June through to the Christopher Nolan film screening on 16 July.
Penelope. Athena. Circe. Helen. Nausicaa. These women have been living in my research for months and this series is the moment I get to go deeper with scholars who’ve spent their careers inside this text.
I’ll be sharing Substack reflections after each session and the first one is free, with sessions 2–5 are for paid subscribers.
Link in bio for the Readings online membership.
In partnership with @readingsbooks

posts.caption:
For Nina Davuluri (@NinaDavuluri) and Ryan Coleman’s wedding in Michigan, guests travelled by ferries and horse carriages before a weekend of South Indian rituals, Broadway performances and family food. Since 2016, the bride had been keeping what she called The Sari Diaries, redesigning Kanjivaram saris belonging to her mother and grandmothers into custom pieces for important moments in her life. Across the weekend, family saris became a tea-length mehendi set, an evening gown and a farewell tux. For the Indian ceremony, Davuluri wore her mother’s emerald and gold Kanjivaram wedding sari from 1981.
Photos: @niravpatelweddings
#VogueWeddings

posts.caption:
People are exhausted by fear in real life, but drawn to fear when it has a set narrative. Horror gathers real anxieties into a story and gives audiences the temporary relief of knowing it’s going to end soon. For an audience trained to soothe itself through tracking devices and grounding routines, something is refreshing about a genre that says fear is real, the room is not fine and your lights probably should not be flickering like that. Click the link in our bio to read more.
Image and video credits: @blumhouse and @imdb
#VogueCulture

posts.caption:
Chaos is something @GracieAbrams has grown accustomed to. Eight years ago, she was a first-year student at Barnard with a cult fandom she’d fostered through Instagram; soon she was signed to Interscope, then on her first tour, then opening for Olivia Rodrigo and Taylor Swift. By the end of 2024, Abrams was a Grammy-nominated pop star filling arenas.
She finally took a breather last summer. “It’s not until you stop that you realize ‘Oh, I haven’t seen my friends in a long time. I haven’t cooked a meal in a long time,’” Abrams says. “The sleep schedule becomes regular for the first time in, like, four years.”
Now 26, she’s shifting into a new gear. Abrams’s third studio album, “Daughter from Hell,” out in July, sounds like her—earnest, introspective, folk-informed—but the production is more lush (“It feels like me in progress,” she says); she’s also in a happy relationship and making her first film, “Please,” with the filmmaker Halina Reijn.
For Vogue’s Summer 2026 issue, Brittany Spanos meets Gracie Abrams in Los Angeles and New York to talk about growing up, slowing down, falling in love, and where she’d like to go next. Tap the link in our bio to read the full profile.
Photographed by @larissahofmann, Written by @brittanyspanos, Styled by @jordenbickham, Hair by @tamastuzes, Makeup by @emikaneko, Manicure by @1.800.nailme, Tailoring by Irina Tshartaryan for Susie’s Custom Designs, Produced by @hyperion.la, Set Design by  @patienceharding. Shot on location @lucasmuseum. Vogue, Summer 2026.

posts.caption: On Friday June 19, New York–based Nigerian-American artist, surrealist photographer, and filmmaker @ChiemekaOffor held her fourth annual Juneteeth Community Photo Booth in Prospect Park. For Offor, images do more than crystallize a moment in time; they express care and form a living archive of Black life. Head to the link in our bio for a conversation between Folasade Ologundudu (@sadeolo) and Offor about this year's photo booth, her photography approach, and what Juneteenth represents.

Head to the link in our bio for more details on the team's looks that captivated social media.

posts.caption: “Sicilian Holidays,” as the @dolcegabbana menswear show was titled, offered a fitting illustration of Stefano Gabbana and Domenico Dolcems culture. Grounded in Dolce and Gabbana’s signature broad-shouldered, assertive tailoring, the collection returned to a territory they have long made their own: unapologetic decoration. While recent seasons had kept embellishment on a tighter leash, here restraint packed its bags and left for a Sicilian summer. Tap the link in bio to see every look and to read the full review from @tiziana_cardini.

posts.caption: @DuaLipa has finally revealed what she wore for her main wedding look: a feather and crystal-embellished @Chanelofficial haute couture gown. The first haute couture wedding dress designed by @Matthieu_Blazy for a friend of the house. The gown was handcrafted in the ateliers at 31, Rue Cambon in Paris, with several contributions by various maisons d’art for its detailed embellishments—entirely hand-embroidered with 480,000 beads by Atelier Montex, and featuring trompe l’oeil jewels requiring 1,155 hours of needlework by Lesage. Callum Turner wore a custom @LouisVuitton suit for the occasion. See more and get all of the details on her gown at the link in bio. Photos: @davidsimsofficial

posts.caption: America's most eminent menswear maestro returned to Milan this evening to present two closely connected collection cohorts: Purple Label, his equivalent of senior year, and Polo, for fresh men.“I loved the oldness, the craftsmanship, the utility that wove together an individuality of ease, eclectic mystique, and romantic sophistication,” said Ralph Lauren on the "gentlemen athletes" who inspired him. Purple Label came out first. Silk-linen tweed tailoring was worn with fisherman sandals, and washed indigo satin jackets sat over tuxedo shirts. Polo was a delightful uprising of reference and genre. From frog-camo pants, field parkas, and fishing shirts to boater hats, rowing blazers, and Bengal-stripe rugby jerseys, a discipline for every gentleman athlete of style was represented on the runway. It was new-school old-school. Tap the link in bio to see every look. Photographs by @styledumonde

posts.alt_text: Colorful, textural, and always on-trend—luxury fashion consultant @roopal_patel knows an It item when she sees one; she’s built her career doing just that. A creature of habit, when she finds something she likes, she sticks with it. (Case in point: Khaite dresses are her style signifier.) Her style in a few words? Bold with a touch of whimsy. Shop her wishlist at the link in bio.

posts.caption: Everyone has their favorite pair of sneakers—whether that be a sporty trainer, a comfortable canvas, or one with a retro feel—but no collection is complete without one (or two!) in white. Whether you’re searching for fashion or function, head to the link in our bio to shop Vogue's edit of the best white sneakers available right now. Photographed by @jancrlo
